blocks.text:
Question-Based Framework.

Clarify why the prospect is there. You need to identify what they need help with. No understanding = no sell. “What made you comment on a post?”

Label them with a problem. What are they struggling with? What's not working? Then confirm you're on the same page. Without identifying the enemy, you cannot win the war. “So what I’m hearing is that ___ is your problem and you’re trying to solve ___, is that right?”
blocks.copy:
Question-Based Framework.

Clarify why the prospect is there. You need to identify what they need help with. No understanding = no sell. “What made you comment on a post?”

Label them with a problem. What are they struggling with? What's not working? Then confirm you're on the same page. Without identifying the enemy, you cannot win the war. “So what I’m hearing is that ___ is your problem and you’re trying to solve ___, is that right?”

blocks.text: Overview their pain. Ask what they've done in the past. Diagnose why it didn't work. Do this until you've gone through all their failures. This automatically positions you as the logical solution. “What have you done so far?” “(optional) How has that worked for you?”
blocks.copy: Overview their pain. Ask what they've done in the past. Diagnose why it didn't work. Do this until you've gone through all their failures. This automatically positions you as the logical solution. “What have you done so far?” “(optional) How has that worked for you?”

blocks.text:
Sell the vacation to your prospect. Identify the top 3 things that will help. Explain why each is important to their success. Confirm they understand why each variable is essential. This helps them understand how you're going to actually get results. We’ll… 1. Pick up your missed calls 2.

Explain away the prospect's concerns. Overcome the three main objections. Blaming circumstances (Time, Money, Fit), Blaming others (Spouse, Partner, Kids, Employees), Blaming self (Avoiding the decision, extrapolating past to present)
blocks.copy:
Sell the vacation to your prospect. Identify the top 3 things that will help. Explain why each is important to their success. Confirm they understand why each variable is essential. This helps them understand how you're going to actually get results. We’ll… 1. Pick up your missed calls 2.

Explain away the prospect's concerns. Overcome the three main objections. Blaming circumstances (Time, Money, Fit), Blaming others (Spouse, Partner, Kids, Employees), Blaming self (Avoiding the decision, extrapolating past to present)

blocks.text: Reinforce the prospect's decision. Once they've paid, you need to prevent buyer's remorse. Give them a huge hit of dopamine to reaffirm their purchase.
blocks.copy: Reinforce the prospect's decision. Once they've paid, you need to prevent buyer's remorse. Give them a huge hit of dopamine to reaffirm their purchase.

blocks.text: Notes: Only Three Things to Say: Questions, Restatements (proof that you were listening), and Short stories (to explain). NEVER paragraphs and NEVER technobabble about random stuff. - Maintain childlike curiosity at all times
blocks.copy: Notes: Only Three Things to Say: Questions, Restatements (proof that you were listening), and Short stories (to explain). NEVER paragraphs and NEVER technobabble about random stuff. - Maintain childlike curiosity at all times

blocks.text: Perfect, excited to share. Essentially its an ai powers tool that handles all the new leads and turns them into booked appointments completely hands off. I'm talking everything, from the quoting call, to the team dispatch, and even all the way down to invoicing, payments, reviews and even retargeting old clients to come back and spend more money at your business.
